title: Describing a Sprained Ankle to a Nurse
summary: A young man visits a walk-in clinic and describes his sprained ankle symptoms to a triage nurse.
topic: describing a sprained ankle at a walk-in clinic

body.speaker: Nurse Lisa
body.text: Hello, I'm Nurse Lisa. What brings you in today?

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Hi, I hurt my ankle this morning. It's quite painful and I can't walk properly.

body.speaker: Nurse Lisa
body.text: I'm sorry to hear that. Can you tell me exactly how it happened?

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: I was playing football and I twisted my ankle when I landed badly after jumping.

body.speaker: Nurse Lisa
body.text: I see. How long ago did this happen?

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: About three hours ago. The swelling started almost immediately.

body.speaker: Nurse Lisa
body.text: On a scale from one to ten, how would you rate the pain right now?

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: I'd say about a seven. It hurts most when I put weight on it.

body.speaker: Nurse Lisa
body.text: Did you hear or feel a popping sound when you twisted it?

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: No, I didn't hear any popping. But the outside of my ankle is bruised and very swollen.

body.speaker: Nurse Lisa
body.text: Have you put any ice on it since the injury?

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Yes, I iced it for twenty minutes when I got home, and I kept my foot raised on a pillow.

body.speaker: Nurse Lisa
body.text: That was the right thing to do. Are you allergic to any medicines, such as ibuprofen?

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: No, I don't have any allergies. Can I take painkillers for this?

body.speaker: Nurse Lisa
body.text: Yes, ibuprofen will help with both the pain and the swelling. We'll also wrap the ankle with a bandage.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Do I need an X-ray to make sure it isn't broken?

body.speaker: Nurse Lisa
body.text: The doctor will examine your ankle and decide if an X-ray is necessary. Please wait in the next room.

sentences.speaker: Nurse Lisa
sentences.text: Hello, I'm Nurse Lisa. What brings you in today?
sentences.translationVi: Xin chào, tôi là Y tá Lisa. Hôm nay bạn đến vì chuyện gì?

sentences.speaker: Tom
sentences.text: Hi, I hurt my ankle this morning. It's quite painful and I can't walk properly.
sentences.translationVi: Xin chào, tôi đau mắt cá chân sáng nay. Nó khá đau và tôi không thể đi bộ bình thường.

sentences.speaker: Nurse Lisa
sentences.text: I'm sorry to hear that. Can you tell me exactly how it happened?
sentences.translationVi: Tôi tiếc khi nghe điều đó. Bạn có thể kể chính xác chuyện gì đã xảy ra không?

sentences.speaker: Tom
sentences.text: I was playing football and I twisted my ankle when I landed badly after jumping.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi đang chơi bóng đá và tôi bị bẹo mắt cá chân khi hạ xuống không đúng tư thế sau khi nhảy.

sentences.speaker: Nurse Lisa
sentences.text: I see. How long ago did this happen?
sentences.translationVi: Tôi hiểu. Chuyện này xảy ra cách đây bao lâu?

sentences.speaker: Tom
sentences.text: About three hours ago. The swelling started almost immediately.
sentences.translationVi: Khoảng ba giờ trước. Vết sưng bắt đầu gần như ngay lập tức.

sentences.speaker: Nurse Lisa
sentences.text: On a scale from one to ten, how would you rate the pain right now?
sentences.translationVi: Trên thang điểm từ một đến mười, bạn đánh giá cơn đau hiện tại là bao nhiêu?

sentences.speaker: Tom
sentences.text: I'd say about a seven. It hurts most when I put weight on it.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi sẽ nói khoảng bảy. Nó đau nhất khi tôi đặt trọng lượng lên.

sentences.speaker: Nurse Lisa
sentences.text: Did you hear or feel a popping sound when you twisted it?
sentences.translationVi: Bạn có nghe hoặc cảm thấy tiếng bốp khi bạn bị bẹo không?

sentences.speaker: Tom
sentences.text: No, I didn't hear any popping. But the outside of my ankle is bruised and very swollen.
sentences.translationVi: Không, tôi không nghe tiếng bốp nào. Nhưng bên ngoài mắt cá chân của tôi bị bầm tím và rất sưng.

sentences.speaker: Nurse Lisa
sentences.text: Have you put any ice on it since the injury?
sentences.translationVi: Bạn có chườm đá lên vết thương từ khi bị chưa?

sentences.speaker: Tom
sentences.text: Yes, I iced it for twenty minutes when I got home, and I kept my foot raised on a pillow.
sentences.translationVi: Có, tôi chườm đá trong hai mươi phút khi về nhà và tôi để chân cao trên gối.

sentences.speaker: Nurse Lisa
sentences.text: That was the right thing to do. Are you allergic to any medicines, such as ibuprofen?
sentences.translationVi: Đó là việc làm đúng. Bạn có dị ứng với thuốc nào không, chẳng hạn như ibuprofen?

sentences.speaker: Tom
sentences.text: No, I don't have any allergies. Can I take painkillers for this?
sentences.translationVi: Không, tôi không có dị ứng nào. Tôi có thể uống thuốc giảm đau không?

sentences.speaker: Nurse Lisa
sentences.text: Yes, ibuprofen will help with both the pain and the swelling. We'll also wrap the ankle with a bandage.
sentences.translationVi: Có, ibuprofen sẽ giúp giảm cả đau lẫn sưng. Chúng tôi cũng sẽ băng mắt cá chân bằng băng.

sentences.speaker: Tom
sentences.text: Do I need an X-ray to make sure it isn't broken?
sentences.translationVi: Tôi có cần chụp X-quang để chắc chắn không bị gãy không?

sentences.speaker: Nurse Lisa
sentences.text: The doctor will examine your ankle and decide if an X-ray is necessary. Please wait in the next room.
sentences.translationVi: Bác sĩ sẽ khám mắt cá chân của bạn và quyết định xem có cần chụp X-quang không. Hãy chờ trong phòng kế tiếp.

hintStarters.text: The pain is … and it started …

hintVocab.meaningVi: đặt trọng lượng lên (chỗ bị đau)

Tôi không thể đi bộ bình thường

annotations.meaningVi: chườm đá trong hai mươi phút

annotations.phrase: kept my foot raised on a pillow
annotations.pronunciation: /kɛpt maɪ fʊt reɪzd ɒn ə ˈpɪloʊ/

annotations.pronunciation: /ðæt wɒz ðə raɪt θɪŋ tuː duː/

annotations.phrase: help with both the pain and the swelling

annotations.pronunciation: /hɛlp wɪð boʊθ ðə peɪn ænd ðə ˈswɛlɪŋ/

annotations.phrase: wrap the ankle with a bandage
annotations.meaningVi: băng mắt cá chân bằng băng

xem có cần chụp X-quang không

grammarNotes.title: Past continuous for interrupted actions: I was playing … when …
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì quá khứ tiếp diễn 'was/were + verb-ing' để diễn đạt hành động đang xảy ra thì bị gián đoạn. Ví dụ: 'I was playing football and I twisted my ankle.' Hành động 'playing' đang diễn ra thì 'twisted' xảy ra.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the past continuous 'was/were + verb-ing' for an action in progress that was interrupted. Example: 'I was playing football and I twisted my ankle.' The ongoing action is 'playing' and the interrupting event is 'twisted'.

grammarNotes.title: Scale phrases: on a scale from … to …
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Cụm 'on a scale from one to ten' dùng để đánh giá mức độ của điều gì đó. Rất hữu ích trong bối cảnh y tế khi mô tả mức độ đau. Ví dụ: 'How would you rate the pain on a scale from one to ten?'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The phrase 'on a scale from one to ten' is used to rate the severity of something. It is very useful in medical contexts to describe pain levels. Example: 'How would you rate the pain on a scale from one to ten?'

criticalThinkingQuestion: What are the first steps you should take when you sprain your ankle, and why is it important to see a medical professional even if the injury seems minor?
